question: According to the data, did the first half or the second half of the year have higher total sales?

options.A: Cannot be determined from the chart.
options.B: Both periods show equal total sales.

explanation: The months on the x‑axis are arranged in reverse (Dec back to Jan), so the filled area shape is misleading if read left‑to‑right. When you re‑order the months to the natural calendar sequence and sum Jan–Jun versus Jul–Dec, you find that the first half actually has higher total sales. The inappropriate month order can easily invert this interpretation.
